title: Phegopteris elongata
taxonomy: Phegopteris elongata is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Phegopteris and is often referred to as a member of the order Polypodiales. Within the family Dryopteridaceae, it is further classified as a part of the subfamily Dryopteridoideae. The classification of Phegopteris elongata is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular studies. Further research is needed to confirm its exact relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Phegopteris elongata, also known as the elongated lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Phegopteris elongata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Phegopteris elongata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and congestion.
